body:
# Week 2026-W23 — Mon 1 Jun to Sun 7 Jun 2026

Second weekly entry. Built 31 May 2026 — feedback half locked at the airport before the Manchester flight, plan half locked the same evening after landing. Follows [[Library/processes/pf-weekly-loop]] including the Voice & framing rules section added 31 May after Pete corrections during the inaugural W22 walk.

> [!info] Feedback on the week-just-finished lives in the closing week's file
> The W22 feedback walked this morning landed in [[Personal/passion-fit/weekly/2026-W22#Reflection-on-the-week|2026-W22.md under ## Reflection on the week]] (matches dashboard `splitWeekEntry()` parser; see [[Library/lessons/2026-05-31-pf-weekly-reflection-lives-in-closing-week-file]]). This W23 file is plan-only.

## This week — plan

### Shape of the week (work + life context)

> Monday is a really important all-day team meeting. It's taken a lot of prep and it's a critical meeting with new contracts starting, and we only have 1 or 2 of these a year. So this is taking all my focus tomorrow: morning prep, meeting, then unfortunately a 4-hour drive to Peterborough straight after. I'll listen to the PF seminar in the car during the drive. It will be a rest day in terms of training.
>
> Following this I have 2 days of actually training others for Sygma. It's been a while. It's an essential 2 days for Clancy, delivering to lots of people. Looking forward to it, but it will be intense with a round-robin format.
>
> Straight after the Wednesday training I drive 2 hours to Stansted then fly home. Late night, hence Wednesday being another rest day.
>
> Continuing in the training theme, swim with Kimbo on Thursday morning, but this is straight after a long travel day and a really late arrival home, so will depend on feel.
>
> The issue always with travelling is a backlog of CD stuff when I get home. I have already tried to pre-empt this by making sure the office books nothing in for me Thursday and Friday.
>
> It's also Austin's birthday this weekend and we are taking a load of kids out.
>
> I already have one eye on next week's second trip, so getting behind with anything will have a knock-on effect.

*Calendar context: Mon team meeting 11:15 + Risk Group 11:30 + car hire 15:00 + hotel check-in Peterborough 17:00 + PF seminar 17:30 (from the car). Tue H&S stand down 08:00 + Zero Strike event 10:00 + 3pm run. Wed H&S stand down 08:00 + car drop Stansted 14:00 + flight ACE 16:10. Thu Open Water swim with Kimbo 09:30. Fri Turbo Intervals 05:40 (also Hctg's birthday). Sat 8am Open Water swim. Sun = Austin's birthday (DoB 2016-06-07).*

### Goals and priorities — at a glance

- **Daily theme:** must-do not want-to-do
- **Daily binary check:** did I get 45 minutes wind-down before sleep, yes or no?
- **Mid-week (by Wed evening):** Tue afternoon run done, Clancy delivered cleanly across Tue and Wed, home Wed without leaving anything undone
- **End-of-week (Sun 7 Jun):** training majority hit (2 swims, indoor bike, 2 runs is a good week), CD backlog from travel days cleared Thu and Fri, Austin's birthday clear and present
- **Lead behaviour:** Control the Controllables, with adaptability as the working partner
- **Energy bottleneck:** 3 days on my feet presenting (Mon meeting + Tue/Wed training delivery), all back-to-back

### Daily theme — fuller

> Must-do not want-to-do. With so much travel and uncertainty, I need to stay in control of what I can and not get influenced by things I can't. The week is going to demand adaptability and ruthless prioritising — small goals, communicate and delegate well, protect my energy. Three days on my feet presenting to people means energy is the bottleneck.

### Behaviour focus

> Control the Controllables, same as last week — but with adaptability as the working partner. The nature of this week means the plan will move; staying in control means choosing what moves and what doesn't, not letting other people's noise pick for me.

### Training plan

| Day | Session | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Mon 1 Jun | REST | Meeting + 4hr drive (PF seminar in the car) + hotel arrival |
| Tue 2 Jun | Run, 3pm | The only training window of the UK leg |
| Wed 3 Jun | REST | Training delivery → 2hr drive → flight ACE 16:10 → late arrival home |
| Thu 4 Jun | Swim, Open Water with Kimbo 09:30 | Feel-dependent — long travel night before |
| Fri 5 Jun | Indoor bike, Turbo Intervals 05:40 | Already on Xhale → calendar |
| Sat 6 Jun | Swim, 8am Open Water | Already on Xhale → calendar |
| Sun 7 Jun | (Optional second run if family time allows) | Austin's birthday — family first |

> A good week is 2 swims, indoor bike, 2 runs. The second run is loose, Sat afternoon or Sun morning depending on Austin's day shape. Anything else is a bonus.

### Commitments to Loren / coach loop

- Continue the daily journal practice through the travel week — test of the W22 discipline under different conditions.
- Maintain the Xhale proactivity (W22's other win — every session posted promptly).
- **Experiment for the week:** even when working late from the hotel, finish in time for 45 minutes wind-down with a book before sleep. Finishing work and going straight to bed = poor sleep (proved W22 Wed). The wind-down is the lever, not the willpower. Tracking daily as a yes/no in the journal.

## Reflection on the week

*To be added end of week (Sat 6 / Sun 7 Jun). Walk: read W23 daily journals + Garmin daily files + dashboard W23 page + Asana closures, walk the framework sections with Pete, capture his prose at fuller length, lock + paste to Xhale, generate W24 plan.*

## Loren's feedback

*(Will be appended when Pete brings comments back from Xhale.)*
